title: chaerophyllum freynii
intro: Chaerophyllum freynii, also known as Fränkel's parsley or Fränkel's chervil, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, Chaerophyllum freynii has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Chaerophyllum freynii can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and it is also used in salads and as a garnish in traditional cuisine.
